text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy hamlet nestled between fertile valleys, there lived a solitary cat named Whiskerbush. Whiskerbush had burgundy fur, piercing green eyes, and a perpetually bored expression. Every morning, he would stretch out on the warm sill outside Ladybird’s cottage, gazing blankly into the distance.

Ladybird was a peculiar sort—a retired opera singer with a penchant for collecting oddities. She’d baked a pie one particularly clear morning sky. It was just the thing to lure a friend into her home.

One sunny afternoon, Whiskerbush sneaked through the door upon Ladybird’s call. There she was, humming a tune. “Look what I’ve found,” she announced triumphantly. A tiny bird, as gray as fog.

“I shall name you Rufus,” said Ladybird, brushing a feather from Rufus’s plumage.

Rufus proved himself an excellent singer with just one lesson, though he still only had one
